mission.title: Install and Configure K8sgpt on Kubernetes
mission.description: K8sGPT is a powerful tool that enhances Kubernetes management by scanning clusters and diagnosing issues using AI. Installing the K8sGPT Operator allows you to leverage AI capabilities to improve your Kubernetes operations.

steps.title: Add the K8sGPT Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official K8sGPT Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add k8sgpt https://charts.k8sgpt.ai
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install K8sGPT Operator using Helm
steps.description:
Install the K8sGPT Operator in a new namespace called 'k8sgpt-operator-system':
```bash
helm install k8sgpt k8sgpt/k8sgpt-operator --namespace k8sgpt-operator-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the K8sGPT Operator pod is running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n k8sgpt-operator-system
```

steps.title: Configure the OpenAI secret
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes secret to store your OpenAI token. Replace `<your-openai-token>` with your actual token:
```bash
kubectl create secret generic k8sgpt-secret --from-literal=secretKey=<your-openai-token> --namespace k8sgpt-operator-system
```

steps.title: Access K8sGPT service
steps.description:
Port-forward the K8sGPT service to access it locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/k8sgpt --namespace k8sgpt-operator-system 8080:8080
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the K8sGPT Operator pod running in the 'k8sgpt-operator-system' namespace, and you will be able to access it via port 8080 on your local machine. Ensure that the OpenAI token is correctly configured in the Kubernetes secret.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add k8sgpt https://charts.k8sgpt.ai
helm repo update
kubectl create secret generic k8sgpt-secret --from-literal=secretKey=<your-openai-token> --namespace k8sgpt-operator-system

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the K8sGPT Operator release from the cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources, but not the CRDs or persistent volumes yet.
```bash
helm uninstall k8sgpt --namespace k8sgpt-operator-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volumes that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources related to K8sGPT are removed from the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete crd k8sgpts.core.k8sgpt.ai
kubectl delete crd results.core.k8sgpt.ai
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace k8sgpt-operator-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the K8sGPT namespace to clean up any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace k8sgpt-operator-system
kubectl get namespaces | grep k8sgpt
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it is a good practice to back up the current state of your K8sGPT installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration.
```bash
helm get values k8sgpt --namespace k8sgpt-operator-system > k8sgpt-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to get the latest charts and then upgrade your K8sGPT installation to the desired version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade k8sgpt k8sgpt/k8sgpt-operator --namespace k8sgpt-operator-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check the status of the K8sGPT Operator pod to ensure it is running the new version without issues.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n k8sgpt-operator-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the K8sGPT pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs to diagnose the issue. This can happen due to misconfiguration or missing secrets.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n k8sgpt-operator-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the K8sGPT service, ensure that the port-forwarding command is running and check if the service is up.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n k8sgpt-operator-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to insufficient resources, check the resource limits and requests set for the K8sGPT Operator deployment. You may need to adjust these values based on your cluster's capacity.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment k8sgpt-operator -n k8sgpt-operator-system
```

troubleshooting.title: OpenAI secret not found
troubleshooting.description:
If K8sGPT cannot access the OpenAI API, verify that the secret containing your OpenAI token exists and is correctly configured.
```bash
kubectl get secret k8sgpt-secret -n k8sgpt-operator-system
kubectl describe secret k8sgpt-secret -n k8sgpt-operator-system
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and have Helm and kubectl installed on your machine.
